body_txt: A Mad Scientist getting distracted will probably just end up in The Madness Place for a while. But for most other characters, the results can be more immediate. Whether it's a Backseat Driver causing you to veer off the road, someone jaw-dropping walking past as you were about to step over a hole, or a startling remark while you have a sharp object in your hand, not paying attention at just the right time can be a serious problem. (And, of course, it will always happen at the right time to make the sufferer run into an upcoming tree , telephone pole, Low Clearance , or Priceless Ming Vase ...) Typically Played for Laughs , especially in slapstick films, but Played for Drama examples are possible — especially if We Need a Distraction from something bigger, if I Shall Taunt You during combat, or if it's a Safe Driving Aesop . Overlaps with Watch Out for That Tree! , which may or may not involve a specific distraction; Magic Misfire , which can easily result from disrupting Concentration-Bound Magic ; Distracted by the Sexy , which will often become dangerous if the one affected is able to do more than just stand and stare; and Failed a Spot Check , which can overlap if someone overlooks a danger to themselves (but failing to notice something unrelated happening to someone else is not usually this trope). A Dramatic Drop can also become dangerous in the quite likely event that the dropped item was fragile , although comedies will often ignore the implied glass shards on the floor . Driver Faces Passenger , Look Both Ways , and Spit Take tend to be Sub Tropes (although technically it is possible for those to happen in ways other than distraction), and examples of them should usually go on those pages. Characters suffering from Attention Deficit... Ooh, Shiny! are particularly prone to this (and tend to do it to themselves without outside help). Not to be confused with Distracted from Death , which is a reaction to death, rather than being the cause. Also note that this trope is specifically about the distraction itself being dangerous; things like someone taking the opportunity to Back Stab you while you're looking away will not normally be in scope, since the danger was separate from the distraction. (It might still count if you were already in the middle of a fight with someone, and unwisely look away from them; in that case, you really did it to yourself.)
